svg.text: Not triggered by a policy change notification
Runs once on initial setup for every policy in the system
Re-runs when a new policy version is added or document changes

g.text: Relational DB — source of record
holds every policy + all supporting documents linked to each policy
each doc record: policy_id, doc_id, doc_title, doc_type, link_type, HTML
Step 1a — Fetch all policies and their documents
input: no filter — retrieve every policy record in the system
for each policy: fetch the main policy doc + all linked supporting docs
each record: policy_id, doc_id, doc_title, doc_type, link_type, HTML
link_type describes the relationship e.g. procedure, standard, reference
Step 1b — Fetch all LOB profiles (runs in parallel with 1a)
input: call to external LOB system — no shared key with Relational DB
output: all LOB names, descriptions, responsibilities, scope
MongoDB — raw inputs stored for this pipeline run
all policy doc records + supporting doc records (link_type preserved)
all LOB profiles from the external system
chunk registry: chunk_id + content_hash for every previously embedded chunk
registry is empty on first run — populated after Step 10
Step 2 — Parse HTML into section tree (every document)
input: HTML content field from each document record in MongoDB
h1/h2/h3 tags become section boundaries — no AI involved
output: section tree — heading path + body text blocks per section
Step 3 — Extract numbered requirements (policy doc only)
input: section tree of the main policy document from Step 2
pattern match finds numbered items: 1.1, 1.2, 2.3 in the body text
output: requirement number + full text, grouped by section
Step 4 — Build chunks (all data streams converge here)
policy sections + supporting doc sections + LOB profiles all produce chunks
Type A
Requirement
chunk
one per req
policy doc only
Type B
Section body
chunk
one per section
all documents
Type C
Supporting doc
section chunk
supporting docs
only

svg.text: Step 5 — Attach metadata fields to every chunk
policy_id: links every chunk back to its parent policy
doc_id: which specific document this chunk came from
parent_doc_id: for supporting doc chunks — points to main policy doc
link_type: how this supporting doc relates e.g. procedure, standard
doc_title + section_heading_path + req_number: passed to LLM at query time
content_hash: fingerprint of chunk text — used to skip re-embedding
used at query time: policy_id = X AND chunk_type IN (B, C)

g.text: Step 6 — Inject context into chunk text
prepend to every chunk: policy title + doc title + section heading path
then a separator line, then the actual content text
makes every chunk self-contained — carries its own context when retrieved
Step 7 — Check content_hash against MongoDB registry
compute a hash of each chunk's text and compare to the registry
hash already exists → chunk unchanged → skip embedding
hash is new or changed → send to embedding model in Step 8
Step 8 — Embed chunks (batched)
input: chunk text for all new or changed chunks
output: a vector (list of numbers) per chunk
Step 9 — Write to Vector DB (upsert)
store each record: vector + full chunk text + all metadata fields together
upsert — updates if chunk_id already exists, inserts if new
Index 1 — Policy index
one physical index
Type A: requirement chunks
Type B: section body chunks
Type C: supporting doc chunks
chunk_type field tells them apart
Index 2 — LOB index
one physical index
LOB profile chunks
one record per LOB
separate — searched
with req text
Step 10 — Update chunk registry in MongoDB
write: chunk_id + content_hash + embedding model + timestamp
on next run Step 7 reads this to skip unchanged chunks
marks chunks as needs_refresh if source document version changed
Vector DB is ready
Index 1 (policy) and Index 2 (LOB) built for all policies
query pipeline can run when a change notification arrives
